element_introduction_order.mastery_context: 8-directional firing established immediately as core mechanic
Vertical-only throw mechanic; used for obstacle clearing and multi-enemy kills
Optional content; enemies transport POWs across screen during progression
Screen stops; soldiers stream from gate; cowardly officer can be shot for bonus points
More powerful than rifle; lost upon death; requires grenade access to bunker
Mitigates resource scarcity; lost upon death
Reveals all hidden bunker locations; lost upon death

typical_relief_triggers: End of level screen lock during gate confrontation phase
typical_tension_triggers: Continuous wave of enemy soldiers throughout level
Gate/fortress finale with massed soldiers streaming from single point
Level repeats with increasing difficulty (NES: 4 designs, 16 total levels)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Relentless forward pressure with escalating difficulty; no safe zones; tension climax at level's end gate confrontation

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Hidden underground shelters accessible only via grenade throws; non-mandatory prisoner rescues during enemy transport sequences
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Off-screen bunkers revealed by special glasses upgrade; grenades required for entry; contain upgrades and rescuable prisoners

difficulty_curve.early_game: Steady introduction of core mechanics (rifle + grenades); manageable enemy density
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Enemy soldier wave intensity increases; level repeats begin with heightened difficulty parameters
difficulty_curve.late_game: Persistent high difficulty; player must survive up to 140 levels total (arcade target); upgrades become critical for progression
notable_spikes: First gate/fortress confrontation (end of Level 1) - introduces forced combat scenario
Transition from unique level 4 to repeated levels with modifications - difficulty parameter changes

notable_level_patterns.description: Standard level structure: continuous horizontal forward movement through enemy-filled jungle with obstacles; screen lock at end for mandatory gate confrontation with streaming soldiers and officer bonus target
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Levels 1-4 (unique designs); pattern repeats 5-16 with difficulty variance

notable_level_patterns.description: Non-mandatory side objective; enemies periodically transport prisoners across screen; player can engage to rescue, earning points without advancing progress
Secret off-screen locations containing machine gun, unlimited grenades, or glasses powerup; must be discovered and accessed with grenades; powerups lost on death
notable_level_patterns.example_level: NES version exclusive; scattered throughout level designs

sources_used: tcrf - Commando (NES) The Cutting Room Floor (gameplay mechanics, NES features, level structure)
wikipedia - Commando (video game) (arcade version context, level count, designer Tokuro Fujiwara, gameplay overview)
